description: General Data Protection Regulation. SidClaw directly supports Article 22 (automated decision-making + human oversight), Article 25 (data protection by design), and Article 30 (records of processing).

mapping.title: Automated individual decision-making — right to human intervention
sidclaw_features: Approval primitive — a data subject decision an AI agent is about to make can be held for human review before execution
Context-rich approval cards give reviewers the agent's reasoning, relevant data, and policy rationale
Risk-based policies can automatically route decisions with legal effect to human approvers

mapping.title: Data protection by design and by default
sidclaw_features: Data classification tiering enforces least-privilege access at the action layer
Agent-scoped permissions — only agents authorized for a classification can access data of that level
Tenant isolation prevents cross-customer data leakage

mapping.title: Records of processing activities
sidclaw_features: Hash-chained audit trace of every agent action — who, what, when, why, outcome
JSON and CSV export for controller / processor records
Webhook event bus for integration with organization-wide RoPA systems

mapping.title: Notification of a personal data breach
sidclaw_features: Drift detection alerts may provide early warning of anomalous behavior
gaps: Breach notification to supervisory authorities is customer-managed — SidClaw does not automate the 72-hour notification

mapping.title: Data protection impact assessment (DPIA)
sidclaw_features: Policy rationale field captures WHY a rule exists — feeds DPIAs
Approval reasoning is archived and exportable
